what_it_does: Regenerates the UI Contract coverage map by parsing contract:op YAML fences in resources/*.md, joining their atlas tags and statuses against atlas/FEATURE-ATLAS.md, computing class/affordance coverage, comparing the result to a hand-derived GROUND_TRUTH anchor, writing coverage/coverage.json, and optionally rewriting only the machine-owned block of COVERAGE.md.
observations: Uses a regex to extract contract:op fences and PyYAML to parse each op's atlas tags and status.
Defines a hard-coded GROUND_TRUTH dict with counts and a covered class set, used as a regression anchor.
Computes closure in both directions: defined-but-not-used (silent gaps) and used-but-not-defined (phantom tags).
Distinguishes building/live ops from planned-only ops when rolling up affordance and class coverage.
Layer-2 DEMONSTRATED is explicitly empty by construction because no op is live and no harness has run.
Parse errors are a hard failure (exit 4) checked before the ground-truth comparison.
--render-md surgically replaces a sentinel-delimited machine block in COVERAGE.md while preserving human prose.
Rendering is deterministic: all sets are sorted and no timestamps are emitted.
standards: The GROUND_TRUTH constant is a deliberate, comment-chronicled regression anchor that must be manually refreshed for intended status flips.
Malformed contract:op fences are treated as a hard failure rather than a warning to prevent silent undercounting.
The markdown renderer uses HTML-comment sentinels to own only the machine-derived headline block and leave prose untouched.
conventions: Follows a standard argparse/pathlib/json/re/yaml CLI tool structure.
Uses global constants for the op-fence regex and the building/live status set.
Prefers deterministic, sorted output for reproducible diffs.
Mixes type hints: some functions are annotated, others are not.
Relies heavily on inline comments to document intent and historical status changes.
concerns: GROUND_TRUTH is hard-coded and can become stale if not deliberately refreshed.
Class/affordance extraction depends on simple regexes over markdown tables and may break if atlas formatting changes.
The first-run --render-md logic replaces the hand-derived headline body; missing expected headings or section breaks causes a loud fatal error.
A skipped YAML fence removes that op's atlas contributions, which the code acknowledges can silently drop affordances from the reached count.
notes: A deterministic coverage auditor that joins contract op fences to the feature atlas, validates against a hand-derived ground-truth anchor, and can regenerate only the machine-owned section of COVERAGE.md.
questions: What is the exact schema and location convention for the resources/*.md files beyond containing contract:op fences?
How is the hand-derived GROUND_TRUTH initially established and who is responsible for refreshing it?
What CI behavior is intended when the tool exits 3 for divergence?
What precise diagnostics does tools/validate_contract.py produce for malformed fences?
purpose_vs_actual: The stated purpose—machine-regenerated coverage with honest divergence reporting, hard parse-error gating, and surgical markdown regeneration—matches the implementation.
apparent_themes: Honest accounting of demonstrated vs contracted coverage layers.
Regression anchoring with deliberate manual refresh.
intention_signals: It is trying to make coverage status an auditable, reproducible artifact that surfaces real drift between the live contract corpus and the human-maintained coverage narrative, without overwriting human prose.
novelty: The combination of a hard-coded, comment-chronicled ground-truth regression anchor, exit-code-driven divergence reporting, and sentinel-delimited surgical markdown regeneration is unusual for a coverage tool.
contribution: Provides a deterministic, self-auditing coverage generator that can emit JSON, compare live corpus joins to a declared ground truth, and update only a machine-owned markdown block while preserving surrounding prose.
summary_for_embedding: A Python tool that parses UI Contract op fences, joins them to a feature atlas, computes class/affordance coverage, compares results to a hand-derived ground truth, and regenerates machine-owned coverage artifacts.
intention_for_embedding: To replace hand-maintained coverage summaries with a reproducible, auditable machine join that loudly reports parse failures and status divergences.

symbols.does: Reads the atlas and resources corpus, extracts defined classes/affordances, out-of-scope affordances, and parses every contract:op YAML fence into per-affordance operation records.
symbols.description: Walks atlas/FEATURE-ATLAS.md and atlas/OUT-OF-SCOPE.md to collect class/affordance identifiers and names, then scans resources/*.md for contract:op fences, parses them with PyYAML, and builds an affordance-to-operations map while recording parse errors.

symbols.does: Computes coverage closure, affordance status roll-up, and class-level covered/planned-only/unmapped buckets.
symbols.description: Compares defined and used affordances in both directions, splits reached affordances by building/live versus planned-only statuses, and rolls those up to class-level coverage buckets using the class prefix of each affordance ID.

symbols.does: Compares computed coverage totals and covered class set against the hard-coded GROUND_TRUTH anchor.
symbols.description: Checks class counts, affordance counts, and the exact covered class set against the GROUND_TRUTH constant, returning a list of divergence messages for the human-readable report and exit-code logic.

symbols.does: Serializes the coverage analysis into a structured JSON object.
symbols.description: Builds the coverage/coverage.json payload including totals, class buckets, closure lists, per-affordance operation details, and an explicit Layer-2 DEMONSTRATED empty-by-construction section.

symbols.does: Prints a human-readable coverage map and ground-truth comparison to stdout.
symbols.description: Emits class and affordance totals, covered/planned-only/unmapped class lists, closure gaps/phantoms, parse errors, and a match/divergence verdict against the hand-derived ground truth.

symbols.does: Formats a sorted list of class identifiers as a compact comma-separated string.
symbols.description: Helper used by the markdown renderer to produce deterministic, sorted class lists for table cells.

symbols.does: Builds the deterministic machine-owned markdown block for COVERAGE.md.
symbols.description: Creates a sentinel-delimited markdown section containing headline counts tables, affordance grain summary, and op count, with all sets sorted and no timestamps so repeated runs are byte-identical.

symbols.does: Surgically replaces the machine block in COVERAGE.md while preserving human-authored prose.
symbols.description: Locates existing HTML-comment sentinels and replaces only the block between them, or on first run inserts the block after the headline heading before the section break; fails loudly if the expected anchors are missing and writes only when content changes.

symbols.does: CLI entry point that orchestrates parsing, coverage computation, reporting, optional artifact writes, and exit-code enforcement.
symbols.description: Parses --corpus, --write-json, and --render-md arguments, invokes the coverage pipeline, prints the map, writes JSON and/or regenerates the markdown machine block, and exits with 0, 3, 4, or 2 depending on match, divergence, parse errors, or fatal conditions.
